title: Super Robot Taisen L featuring NME
display_title: Super Robot Taisen L featuring NME
wikitext:
[]: Super Robot Taisen L featuring NME Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Sep 26, 2010 8:42 pm Since there's no thread around I'll make one. The new Super Robot Taisen L is going to include the characters and units from NME, so far the EVA-01 using his long machine gun, Sachiel identified as 4th angel, Samshel in NME version and Asuka using her "Inazuma kick" have been shown. I wonder what they'll do about Mari especially if it will be possible to keep the EVA-05 (either in his incomplete form or in an hypothetical completed form) or if she'll keep the EVA-02 (and hypothetically Asuka could keep the EVA-03 or maybe she could simply be removed from your team, it was common for Rei after the fight against Armisaelf in old SRW like Alpha or MX) or if she'll never join your team and you'll just use her twice (for the fighting against "Tunniel" and Zeruel); actually so far she's not been shown yet but I doubt that she won't be included. Yet I'm surprised that they decided to include the new movies even if they're not completed (they still didn't include Gundam 00 that is a Bandai series even though it's quite complete even if you don't consider the movie), probably they're going to give some original conclusion to the events and I wonder if you'll fight against the EVA Mark 06 (or maybe even use it, but this is quite hard) or if they'll give up about using it (it's even harder that they'll use the EVA-08 since it's an unit that has not been shown in the movie except in the preview and at least the Mark 6 was shown in those few final seconds and his pilot has been identified). Some screenshoots from the TGS video: TGS video links from SRWHotnews Officially the game should be out in 25 November of this year for the Nintendo DS. Anyway are there SRW fans here in EGF? I used to be one in the past but I kinda disliked the last SRW for DS and this one actually seems quite similar (but the inclusion of Eva and Macross Frontier will probably be a good enough reason to play it).

[]: Re: Super Robot Taisen L featuring NME Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Sep 27, 2010 6:08 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Since there's no thread around I'll make one.

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Sep 27, 2010 10:41 am My bad, I suppose that my oculist was wrong, a pair of months ago I checked my eyes and he said that they were fine; please delete the topic.
